methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Cohesive Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by David Pietrocola that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws on a sector universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), though narrow-filter peer matching within Robotics & Manufacturing yielded insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints to support median peer multiples methodology.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach, grounding valuation in observed Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiples. Using the $5M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 2.5x multiplier yields $12.5M implied valuation. This reflects typical venture progression dynamics for early-stage manufacturing technology companies.

The triangulated analysis, derived from one method, supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median of $12M. Key uncertainties stem from the limited peer-set depth; broader private robotics comparables exist (Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity), but stage and valuation alignment with Cohesive remains unclear from available data. The single public comparable provides limited directional validation. Narrower precision would require either additional same-stage private transaction data or clearer product-market positioning relative to identified peers. This range should be treated as indicative pending deeper due diligence on burn rate, technical differentiation, and go-to-market traction.
